  "clarification_question": "For this section review, should the focus be (A) sentence-/paragraph-level writing polish, (B) logical and claim–evidence consistency of the arguments as stated, or (C) higher-level framing and section structure? Please pick one (or specify a blend).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "Verify the internal logic of the scaling arguments and mechanism claims (e.g., fleet-size invariance, structural properties).",
    "R1.3": "Check that each stated conclusion is supported by the described experimental observations within the text.",
    "R1.6": "Ensure clarity, precision, and readability at the sentence and paragraph level without altering section-level content.",
    "RX.1": "Surface limits and uncertainties (e.g., absence of figures/tables here and training-distribution boundaries) when judging claims.",
    "RX.2": "Avoid endorsing overgeneralizations from N=200 checkpoints or attributing universality to mechanisms without support.",
    "RX.3": "Keep the review strictly confined to this provided section and what it asserts.",
    "RX.4": "Avoid recommendations or interpretations that could mislead deployment or safety-relevant decisions based on overclaims.",
    "RX.5": "Anchor any critique or praise to specific sentences and claims from the provided text for traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: For this section review, should the focus be (A) sentence-/paragraph-level writing polish, (B) logical and claim–evidence consistency of the arguments as stated, or (C) higher-level framing and section structure? Please pick one (or specify a blend).

rationale.R1.2: Verify the internal logic of the scaling arguments and mechanism claims (e.g., fleet-size invariance, structural properties).
rationale.R1.3: Check that each stated conclusion is supported by the described experimental observations within the text.
rationale.R1.6: Ensure clarity, precision, and readability at the sentence and paragraph level without altering section-level content.
rationale.RX.1: Surface limits and uncertainties (e.g., absence of figures/tables here and training-distribution boundaries) when judging claims.
rationale.RX.2: Avoid endorsing overgeneralizations from N=200 checkpoints or attributing universality to mechanisms without support.
rationale.RX.3: Keep the review strictly confined to this provided section and what it asserts.
rationale.RX.4: Avoid recommendations or interpretations that could mislead deployment or safety-relevant decisions based on overclaims.
rationale.RX.5: Anchor any critique or praise to specific sentences and claims from the provided text for traceability.
